sections.title: GENERAL & STAFF DISCRETION
sections.source: These rules override everything. GM, Senior Admin, and Owner's word is final.

charges.name: Admin Discretion
charges.body: Sometimes something happens that doesn't cleanly fall under any other rule on this page. This is that rule. If a situation arises and no other category covers it, any GM can use this section to take action — including a ban. Not everything can be predicted and written down ahead of time. If what you did was bad enough that a GM felt the need to act, this is the charge.
charges.classification: GM Discretion — up to Permanent Ban

charges.name: Loophole Abuse
charges.body: Exploiting a gap in the rules that isn't explicitly covered. "It doesn't say I can't" is not a defense. If you have to twist the wording to justify what you did, you already know it was wrong.
charges.classification: GM Discretion — up to Permanent Ban

charges.name: Repeated Minor Offenses
charges.body: Stacking up small violations that individually are warnings but together show a pattern. If you're getting warned every session, the problem isn't the rules — it's you.

charges.name: Encouraging Others to Break Rules
charges.body: You didn't do it yourself, but you talked someone else into it. Instigating is just as bad as doing it. If someone breaks a rule because you pushed them into it, you're both getting hit.

charges.name: Rule Evasion
charges.body: Finding creative ways to technically not break a rule while clearly violating its intent. We're not lawyers and this isn't a courtroom. If the spirit of the rule is broken, you're in violation.
charges.classification: GM Discretion — up to Permanent Ban

charges.name: Wasting Staff Time
charges.body: Filing false reports, opening frivolous tickets, or repeatedly pulling GMs into non-issues. Staff time is limited — don't burn it on nonsense.
examples: Opening tickets to argue about valid punishments
Repeatedly pinging staff for non-urgent issues

sections.title: RESPECT & CONDUCT
sections.source: The #1 rule. Respect is given unconditionally — until you give us a reason not to.

charges.name: Disrespect Toward Staff / GMs
charges.body: Being disrespectful, aggressive, or confrontational toward staff or GMs. They're volunteering their time to keep the server running. Treat them accordingly.
examples: Insulting or mocking a GM during or after a ruling
Being aggressive when staff is correcting your behavior

charges.name: Disrespect Toward Players
charges.body: Mild to extreme aggressive behavior or disrespect toward other players. We're all here to play — keep it civil.
examples: Targeted harassment or insults in voice/text
Extreme aggressive behavior toward other players
Worked-up player who needs to de-escalate

charges.name: Being Non-Receptive
charges.body: Being argumentative about the rules or refusing to accept corrective action from staff. If staff tells you to stop, you stop. Arguing about it makes it worse.
examples: Rules lawyering — "technically the rules don't say..."
Continuing the behavior after being told to stop

charges.name: Non-English Communication
charges.body: Not speaking English or refusing to communicate in English on the server. We need everyone on the same page for coordination.

charges.name: Inappropriate Names
charges.body: Offensive, racist, sexual, or otherwise inappropriate player names, clan tags, or profile pictures. You'll be asked to change it. If you don't, you're out.

sections.title: RADIO & COMMUNICATIONS
sections.source: Channel 48 is the tactical global channel. Keep it clean.

charges.name: Misuse of Channel 48
charges.body: Using Channel 48 for anything other than tactical communication.

charges.name: Blasting Audio
charges.body: Playing music, soundboards, or other non-tactical audio over comms. This is a warzone, not your Spotify playlist.

charges.name: Faking Staff Authority
charges.body: Making announcements or issuing orders over Channel 48 while pretending to be staff. Only admin announcements are authorized on 48.

sections.title: TEAMKILLING
sections.source: Check your targets. Friendly fire isn't friendly.

charges.name: Mass Teamkilling / TK + Logoff
charges.body: Killing multiple teammates intentionally, or teamkilling and immediately logging off. If intent is unclear on a TK-and-logoff, you may appeal — but mass TK is permanent, no questions asked.
charges.classification: Permanent Ban (appeal if intent unclear on logoff)

charges.name: Intentional Teamkilling
charges.body: Deliberately killing a teammate. Includes overdosing players with medical supplies.
examples: Overdosing a player with medical items
Aggressive trolling resulting in teammate deaths

charges.name: Retaliatory Teamkilling
charges.body: Killing a teammate because they killed you or did something you didn't like. Two wrongs don't make a right — open a ticket instead.

charges.name: Uncoordinated CAS Causing TKs
charges.body: Calling in or executing a CAS strike without proper coordination, resulting in friendly casualties.
examples: CAS on an active point resulting in GI deaths

charges.name: Accidental / Negligent TKs
charges.body: Multiple accidental teamkills from failure to PID. Accidents happen, but if it keeps happening, it's negligence.
examples: Multiple accidental TKs in a session
Failure to identify targets before engaging

charges.name: Revenge TK After Respawn
charges.body: Spawning back in and hunting down the person who killed you. Doesn't matter if they TK'd you first — you respawned, it's over. Open a ticket if you have a problem.

sections.title: VEHICLES & ASSETS
sections.source: If it's not yours, don't touch it.

charges.name: Intentional Vehicle Theft
charges.body: Knowingly taking a vehicle that's claimed by or assigned to another player or squad.

charges.name: Unintentional Vehicle Theft
charges.body: Taking a vehicle you didn't realize was claimed. New players get some grace, but learn the system and ask.

charges.name: Vehicle Destruction / Griefing
charges.body: Deliberately destroying, decommissioning, or wasting vehicles and structures.

charges.name: Misuse of Air Assets
charges.body: Using helicopters and air assets improperly.
examples: Ignoring CAS calls when assigned CAS role

sections.title: GAMEPLAY & OBJECTIVES
sections.source: Play the objective. Don't waste everyone's time.

charges.name: Spawning Unnecessary AI
charges.body: Flying or traveling to non-frontline objectives and spawning in AI unnecessarily.

charges.name: Griefing
charges.body: Any deliberate action taken to disrupt, sabotage, or ruin the game for other players.

charges.name: Trolling
charges.body: Disruptive trolling behavior that affects other players' experience.
examples: Grabbing heli controls causing a crash
Crashing vehicles on purpose resulting in TKs

sections.title: DISCRIMINATION & HATE SPEECH
sections.source: No racism of any kind. This includes Vietnam-era slang. Zero tolerance.

charges.name: Hard Racial Slurs
charges.body: Use of hard racial slurs or remarks, including those directed at other players. There is no warning. There is no appeal. You're done.

charges.name: Mild Racial Slurs (Undirected)
charges.body: Use of mild racial slurs not directed at another player. First-time and receptive = strike. Otherwise = ban.

charges.name: Coded Discrimination
charges.body: Dog whistles, coded language, or edgelord behavior. No grey area. If it smells like racism, it's racism.

charges.name: Offensive Roleplay
charges.body: Using "it's roleplay" or "it's historically accurate" as an excuse for racist, sexist, or otherwise discriminatory behavior. Roleplay is not a shield. If the content would violate any other rule in this section, the RP excuse doesn't save you.

sections.title: DRAMA & TOXICITY
sections.source: Keep it out of the servers. Handle it in tickets.

charges.name: Starting Drama
charges.body: Deliberately stirring up conflict or escalating problems in public channels. Open a ticket for player issues, talk to a Senior Admin for staff issues.

charges.name: Public Complaints
charges.body: Airing grievances in public channels instead of using proper channels. Drama stays out of the servers.

charges.name: Persistent Negativity
charges.body: A consistent pattern of complaining or toxic attitude that drags the community down. Not one bad day — a pattern.

charges.name: Advertising
charges.body: Promoting other servers, communities, or Discord groups in our channels or in-game. This isn't a billboard. First time is a warning. If you keep doing it, you're gone.
examples: Posting invite links to other servers
Recruiting for other communities in-game or in Discord

charges.name: Recruiting Against EoH
charges.body: Actively trying to pull players away from EoH to another community. This isn't about people naturally moving on — it's about deliberately poaching. If you're DMing our members trying to get them to leave, you're done.

charges.name: Squad & Group Policy
charges.body: In-game squads are permitted within EoH as a way for players to group up and enjoy the server together (e.g., Medcom, Ramblowns Medical School, Terry's Medics, Air Cav). However, creating or promoting external groups that pull players away from our Discord or servers is not allowed, as it can lead to unnecessary drama and moderation challenges. Squad promotion is allowed in moderation, but excessive advertising, spamming, or actively pressuring players — especially in-game at MOB — will not be tolerated. These guidelines help us maintain the quality, structure, and safe environment that EoH is built on.
examples: Creating external Discords or groups that compete with EoH
Spamming squad recruitment in-game or in channels
Pressuring players at MOB to join your squad

sections.title: EXPLOITS & CHEATING
sections.source: Don't abuse bugs. Don't cheat. Report what you find.

charges.name: Cheating
charges.body: Using any external cheating software, scripts, or tools. Not a conversation. Not a warning.

charges.name: Bug Abuse
charges.body: Knowingly using a bug, glitch, or unintended feature to gain an advantage or break gameplay.

charges.name: Sharing Exploits
charges.body: Spreading knowledge of bugs, crash methods, or exploits to other players instead of reporting them.

charges.name: Hiding Exploits
charges.body: Finding a bug or exploit and keeping it to yourself instead of reporting it through proper channels.

charges.name: Ban Evasion / Alt Accounts
charges.body: Rejoining the server on a different account after being banned. Doesn't matter how long ago the ban was. If we find out, both accounts are done.

sentencing.sentence: You're done. Immediate removal. Some offenses allow appeal, others don't.
Serious offense. Out for 1-2 weeks minimum depending on severity.
Timed ban. Length scales with the offense. This is the minimum.
Final chance. Next offense in this category is a ban. Minimum.
First-time heads up. Be receptive and correct the behavior. Minimum.

aggravating: Being confrontational, rude, or aggressive when staff addresses you — auto-escalates one level
Repeat offenses — each repeat escalates to the next punishment tier
Multiple offenses in a single session
Zero remorse or doubling down on the behavior
Being told to stop and doing it anyway
Rules lawyering during the disciplinary process (see § 1.3)
